bear_case: The bear case is against the confidence of our own detection, not against the no-call. Two of our three signals are cheap. Keyword density catches bad copywriting, which correlates with a non-technical person having written the deck and not with fraud. Commit-substance ratio would flag any team that squashes on merge, formats in CI, or vendors a dependency. We are one hop from a detector that reliably punishes founders who do not know how our metrics work. Only the injection is decisive - and if a founder's growth consultant added that line to slide 7 without them knowing, we have permanently blacklisted someone for a contractor's decision, on evidence we never showed them.
weakest_evidence: Keyword density 0.41 measures the writing, not the company. Plenty of real infrastructure gets sold with terrible copy.
The commit-substance measure is new and has been validated against exactly one control. One control is an anecdote.
'README describes eleven subsystems, four directories are empty' describes most pre-launch repositories at some point in their life.
We have not established that the founder authored or knew about the slide 7 text.
load_bearing_claim: That the slide 7 injection was placed deliberately by someone acting for this company. Strip that, and what remains is a badly written deck over a thin repository - a pass, not a flag, and certainly not a permanent record.

control_check.detail: 3,402 commits over 11 days - more than Synthgrid's 3,117 over 9 - with 61,400 net lines, 611 tests and 14 external contributors. Zero integrity flags.
control_check.conclusion: The detector is not a volume meter. That is one control, which is better than zero and worse than a validation set.

what_would_change_my_mind: Provenance on the slide 7 text - PDF metadata showing which tool and which author inserted that text layer.
The founder's response when shown the extracted string. That response is more informative than everything else in this file combined.
Ten more controls. Until then the substance metric is a demo, not a detector.
process_objection: We should show the founder the extracted injection string and record the answer before the flag becomes permanent.
